query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Diva', 'Any female celebrity, usually a well known singer or actress', 'Scholars noted a striking diva between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divagation', 'Straying off from a course or way', 'Her divagation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Diverge', 'To separate and go in different directions', 'Over time, the community learned to diverge its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divergence', 'The act of moving apart or differing', '“There was a divergence of opinions in the debate.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divergent', 'Developing in different directions', '“Their career paths became increasingly divergent.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Diverse', 'Showing a great deal of variety', 'The passage presents a diverse portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Diversion', 'An instance of turning something aside from its course', 'Her diversion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Diversity', 'The quality of being different', 'Scholars noted a striking diversity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divert', 'To turn aside from a course', 'Critics argued that the plan might divert the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divertible', 'Capable of being diverted', 'Her divertible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divest', 'To undress, disrobe', 'The author hoped the new policy would divest tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divination', 'The practice of foretelling the future by supernatural means', 'Her divination became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divine', 'To foretell (something), especially by the use of divination', 'Over time, the community learned to divine its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divinity', 'The property of being divine, of being like a god or god', 'The essay highlights the divinity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divisible', 'Capable of being divided without a remainder', 'Scholars noted a striking divisible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divisive', 'Having a quality that divides or separates', 'The reviewer described the tone as divisive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divisor', 'A number or expression that another is to be divided by', 'Over time, the community learned to divisor its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divulge', 'To make known private information', 'The author hoped the new policy would divulge tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Divulgence', 'The act of making known (private or sensitive information)', 'Scholars noted a striking divulgence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Docile', 'Yielding to control or supervision, direction, or management', 'The author hoped the new policy would docile tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Docket', 'A calendar or list of cases for trial', 'Scholars noted a striking docket between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Doctrine', 'A belief or tenet, especially about theological matters', 'The essay highlights the doctrine of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Doe', 'The female of the deer, antelope, and certain other animals', 'Scholars noted a striking doe between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Doff', 'To remove a hat as a greeting or mark of respect', 'Over time, the community learned to doff its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dogg', 'Persistent in effort, stubbornly tenacious', 'The essay highlights the dogg of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Doggerel', 'Verse or words that are badly written or expressed', 'Scholars noted a striking doggerel between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dogma', 'An official system of tenets, laid down by an authority', 'The essay highlights the dogma of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dogmatic', 'Asserting opinions or beliefs as if they were indisputable facts', 'Her dogmatic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dogmatize', 'To treat something as dogma', 'The author hoped the new policy would dogmatize tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Doleful', 'Mournful, bringing feelings of sadness', 'The passage presents a doleful portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dolesome', 'Doleful, dismal, gloomy', 'Her dolesome became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dolor', 'Sorrow, grief, misery or anguish', 'Her dolor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dolorous', 'Feeling or expressing great sorrow or distress', 'The passage presents a dolorous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dolt', 'A stupid person', 'The essay highlights the dolt of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Domain', 'A geographic area owned by a single person or organization', 'Her domain became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Domestic', 'Of or relating to the home', 'Over time, the community learned to domestic its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Domesticity', 'Affection for the home and its material comforts', 'Scholars noted a striking domesticity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Domicile', 'A home or residence', 'Scholars noted a striking domicile between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dominance', 'Power and influence over others', 'Scholars noted a striking dominance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dominant', 'Most important, powerful, or influential', 'The essay highlights the dominant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dominate', 'To govern, rule or control by superior authority or power', 'Over time, the community learned to dominate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Domination', 'The exercise of control or influence over something', 'Scholars noted a striking domination between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Domineer', 'To rule over or control arbitrarily or arrogantly', 'Critics argued that the plan might domineer the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Don', 'To put on (clothes)', 'Over time, the community learned to don its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Donator', 'One who donates', 'Scholars noted a striking donator between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Donee', 'Someone who receives a gift from a donor', 'Her donee became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Donor', 'One who donates, typically, money', 'Scholars noted a striking donor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dormant', 'Inactive or temporarily not in use', '“The volcano has been dormant for centuries.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dotard', 'A person with impaired intellect, especially an old person', 'Her dotard became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Doublet', 'A close-fitting jacket, men''s wear in the renaissance', 'The essay highlights the doublet of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
